zh.homeIntro1: 《女巫预言》（古诺尔斯语：Völuspá，意为「女先知的预言」）是北欧《诗体埃达》（Poetic Edda）的开篇之作，也是北欧神话最重要的文献之一。全诗约66节，由一位沃尔瓦（völva，女先知/女巫）向主神奥丁讲述世界的起源、历史与终结。
zh.homeIntro2: 诗中描述了世界从虚空（金伦加鸿沟）中创生，经历诸神的黄金时代，到第一场战争打破和平，再到诸神黄昏（Ragnarök）——世界在烈火与洪水中毁灭。但毁灭并非终点：大地将从海中重新升起，幸存的神祇将开启新的时代。
zh.homeIntro3: 该诗约成文于公元950年前后的冰岛，现存于两份中世纪手稿：《皇家手稿》（Codex Regius，约1270年）和《豪克之书》（Hauksbók，约1334年）。本站按诗歌结构分段收录，附白话翻译与文化解读。
zh.coreQuote: 「兄弟相残，姐妹之子互相杀戮；世道险恶，淫风肆虐——斧之时代，剑之时代，盾牌碎裂；风之时代，狼之时代，直到世界沉沦。」
keyPointsList: 创世 —— 世界从金伦加鸿沟中诞生，诸神造天地万物
黄金时代 —— 诸神在伊达平原建殿，万物和谐
第一场战争 —— 华纳神族与阿萨神族开战，和平终结
人类诞生 —— 奥丁等三神用木头创造人类
诸神黄昏 —— 芬里尔挣脱锁链，世界在烈火中毁灭
世界重生 —— 大地从海中升起，新的黄金时代开始
sourceList: 诗歌原文基于《皇家手稿》（Codex Regius）版本，参考 Wikipedia: Völuspá
英译参考 Henry Adams Bellows (1936) 与 Carolyne Larrington (2014) 的学术译本（Sacred Texts）
白话翻译与文化解读为编者整理，不代表学术定论
站点图标采用北欧如尼文字 Algiz (ᛉ)——古日耳曼语中意为「保护」，是北欧文化中最具辨识度的符文之一

en.homeIntro1: Völuspá (Old Norse: 'Prophecy of the Seeress') is the opening poem of the Poetic Edda and one of the most important sources of Norse mythology. Comprising approximately 66 stanzas, it recounts a völva (seeress) telling Odin about the creation, history, and ultimate fate of the world.
en.homeIntro2: The poem describes the world's creation from the primordial void (Ginnungagap), through the gods' golden age, the first war that shattered the peace, and finally Ragnarök — the destruction of the world in fire and flood. But destruction is not the end: the earth rises again from the sea, and surviving gods usher in a new age.
en.homeIntro3: The poem was likely composed around 950 AD in Iceland. It survives in two medieval manuscripts: the Codex Regius (~1270) and Hauksbók (~1334). This site presents the poem in structured sections with modern translations and cultural analysis.
en.coreQuote: "Brothers shall fight and slay each other; sisters' sons shall kinship stain. Hard is it on earth — an age of axes, an age of swords, shields are cloven; an age of storms, an age of wolves, ere the world falls."
keyPointsList: Creation —— The world is born from Ginnungagap, gods shape heaven and earth
Golden Age —— The gods build halls on Iðavöllr, all things in harmony
First War —— The Vanir and Æsir clash, peace is shattered
Human Creation —— Odin and two gods create mankind from driftwood
Ragnarök —— Fenrir breaks free, the world burns in fire
World Renewed —— Earth rises from the sea, a new golden age begins
sourceList: Poem text based on the Codex Regius version, referencing Wikipedia: Völuspá
English translations reference Henry Adams Bellows (1936) and Carolyne Larrington (2014) scholarly editions (Sacred Texts)
Vernacular translations and cultural commentary are editorial, not academic consensus
Site icon: Algiz rune (ᛉ) — meaning 'protection' in Proto-Germanic, one of the most recognizable symbols of Norse culture
